speaker: interviewer
text: Tape what? It's not six. Tape five, August 7th, 1996, Michael Lipenholtz. Can you tell us who is in these pictures?

speaker: interviewee
text: This-- the-- the guy with the hair, the long hair, with the-- it's me. This is my brother.

speaker: interviewer
text: What was your brother's name?

speaker: interviewee
text: Moshe.

speaker: interviewer
text: And what happened to him?

speaker: interviewee
text: He got a-- they took him away to-- to the concentration camp in Estonia. And he died over there, escaping from the-- from the Germans.

speaker: interviewer
text: Do--

speaker: interviewee
text: He got shot.

speaker: interviewer
text: How old was he in this picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: Maybe he was seven.

speaker: interviewer
text: And how old were you?

speaker: interviewee
text: I-- I was there-- [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] --three or four.

speaker: interviewer
text: [INAUDIBLE] Who is this a photo of?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is the photo of my family.

speaker: interviewer
text: And when was it--

speaker: interviewee
text: Taken-- taken in '21. Yeah.

speaker: interviewer
text: Who is this, on the bottom left?

speaker: interviewee
text: Bottom left is me, Michael. My grandpa-- father.

speaker: interviewer
text: What was his name?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yaakov Yosef. [LAUGHS] Hey, I knew it.

speaker: interviewer
text: [LAUGHTER] Was that your father's father?

speaker: interviewee
text: This was my father's father, yeah.

speaker: interviewer
text: And this?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is my father.

speaker: interviewer
text: And standing next to your father?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is my mother.

speaker: interviewer
text: And what was her name?

speaker: interviewee
text: Sonia Bisckis.

speaker: interviewer
text: And what was your father's name?

speaker: interviewee
text: Chaim Avraham. This is my grandmother.

speaker: interviewer
text: And what-- what was your grandmother's name?

speaker: interviewee
text: You got me. [LAUGHS]

speaker: interviewer
text: All right, and who's the boy standing next to your grandmother?

speaker: interviewee
text: Next to my grandmother, sitting, the-- let me see. Moshe.

speaker: interviewer
text: He was your--

speaker: interviewee
text: Well, my brother.

speaker: interviewer
text: Who is this a picture of?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is a picture of me and my brother, taken in Vilna, before the war.

speaker: interviewer
text: Which one is you?

speaker: interviewee
text: I am the smaller one.

speaker: interviewer
text: Who is this a picture of?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is a picture of my parents.

speaker: interviewer
text: And when was it taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: Before the war.

speaker: interviewer
text: How did you get these pictures?

speaker: interviewee
text: Got this picture from my aunt and uncle in Akron, Ohio. That's America. [LAUGHS]

speaker: interviewer
text: Who's the young man in the uniform here?

speaker: interviewee
text: The young man in the uniform, it's me.

speaker: interviewer
text: And what kind of uniform is that?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is from the Polish Army, for the cavalry.

speaker: interviewer
text: And who's standing there with you?

speaker: interviewee
text: My father.

speaker: interviewer
text: And what year was this taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: In--

speaker: interviewer
text: '39, I think.

speaker: interviewer
text: Who is this a picture of?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is a picture of my nephew in the ghetto.

speaker: interviewer
text: What was his name?

speaker: interviewee
text: [? Josef. ?]

speaker: interviewer
text: How old was he in the picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: Three years old.

speaker: interviewer
text: And what happened to him?

speaker: interviewee
text: He got killed by the Germans.

speaker: interviewer
text: Who is this a picture of?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is a picture I took with-- with a group from the-- from the first [NON-ENGLISH]. This is not my [NON-ENGLISH]. You know, [NON-ENGLISH] is a group. And I know them, but if--

speaker: interviewer
text: You're wearing a uniform. What is that from?

speaker: interviewee
text: This-- well, we didn't have no uniform. Everyone had a-- a different something, clothing.

speaker: interviewer
text: Who were these men with you?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is my friends. I knew them from-- from before. And then they belonged to a second [NON-ENGLISH].

speaker: interviewer
text: And when was this picture taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: This picture was take-- where? What-- what is the date?

speaker: interviewer
text: This was taken-- in Vilna.

speaker: interviewee
text: In Vilna.

speaker: interviewer
text: Can you tell us when?

speaker: interviewee
text: After the liberation.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where are you in this picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: I'm this-- the second from the right. [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: And what was it taken? This was taken near-- by the Bisckis house. Now you know it. Who was that?

speaker: interviewee
text: This was the-- had a house, a big house. And in this-- and they were living, this group of protestants were living in the house. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: And you know want to know who the other people are? Who is this a picture of?

speaker: interviewee
text: That's our-- I'm on their gray horse. And this is a Jewish guy. And the other one is a Jewish guy from Vilna. They-- we were the only-- the-- the Jewish guys in the-- in the [INAUDIBLE], it's good enough.

speaker: interviewer
text: When was this taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: This was taken-- taken-- the-- the-- the Polish government was still there.

speaker: interviewer
text: '45. '45. Would you say, was it about 1945?

speaker: interviewer
text: Was this when you were in the--

speaker: interviewee
text: In the army.

speaker: interviewer
text: In the-- in the army. Oh, so that would put it earlier then.

speaker: interviewee
text: That's right.

speaker: interviewer
text: That would be about 1939?

speaker: interviewee
text: '40, maybe. [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS] This is a document from the Russian authorities after the liberation, that I was a-- a commanding officer in-- in the Partisans.

speaker: interviewer
text: Could you tell us about this book?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is the book about the Partisans. It's mentioned about me a couple times. And the-- about us. And our-- it's written by Chaim Lazar. [BACKGROUND NOISE]

speaker: interviewee
text: This is my uncle and aunt from Akron, Ohio. What were their names?

speaker: interviewee
text: I don't know if I got it. They send me the papers to come to United States.

speaker: interviewer
text: Is this Uncle Morris and Aunt-- and Aunt Doris, that you told me about.

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes.

speaker: interviewee
text: What?

speaker: interviewer
text: Is this the aunt and uncle that you told me about, that brought you to America?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah. But I've forgotten their name. What?

speaker: interviewer
text: Who is this a picture of?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is a picture of me and my wife.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where was it taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: This was taken in the United States.

speaker: interviewer
text: Do you remember when? [LAUGHS]

speaker: interviewee
text: '51? Good.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. Who is this a picture of?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is a picture of my son and my son-in-law. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: Yeah? What is-- what are their names?

speaker: interviewee
text: Charles and Diana.

speaker: interviewer
text: Who were these young boys?

speaker: interviewee
text: These youngs boys, are my grandkids.

speaker: interviewer
text: What are their names?

speaker: interviewee
text: Danny and Eric. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewee
text: This is Shelley and Steve. My-- mine son-in-law and my daughter-in-law. She-- she-- you want to tell them? The-- the-- the-- This is your daughter, Shelley. [LAUGHS]

speaker: interviewee
text: This is Jacklyn and Ashley, my daughter's kids. OK? This is me in my Partisans uniform. Thank you very much. [LAUGHS]

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you very much.

speaker: interviewee
text: Thank you, everybody.
